baseName: caps standalone vitest below parent-ledger grants

current.name: gives standalone Vitest the same ledger-governed cap as parent grants

rationale: The standalone cap assertion was deliberately strengthened for the no-queue scheduler: standalone Vitest now receives the same ledger-governed cap as an orchestrated parent rather than the obsolete single-process throttle. The replacement still asserts the base declaration's ledger cap boundary while pinning the updated invariant.

baseName: planLaneConcurrency never raises a lane above the per-process fork cap

current.name: never oversubscribes a grant and queues only when fewer workers than jobs exist

rationale: The replacement checks every planned job allocation against the grant and therefore retains the per-process/no-oversubscription bound under the six-job scheduler.

baseName: planLaneConcurrency keeps instantaneous forks (maxConcurrent*perLane) within the grant

current.name: never oversubscribes a grant and queues only when fewer workers than jobs exist

rationale: The replacement directly sums active job workers and asserts the total never exceeds the ledger grant, preserving the instantaneous-fork invariant for the current heterogeneous lane plan.

baseName: REGRESSION: the single-process throttle (grant=2) collapses lanes to serial

current.name: never oversubscribes a grant and queues only when fewer workers than jobs exist

rationale: The obsolete fixed three-lane reproduction became the low-grant branch of the generalized scheduler test, which still proves constrained grants queue jobs rather than oversubscribing.

baseName: FIX: the fair orchestrator grant runs the 3 lanes concurrently

current.name: allocates eight workers across core, fidelity, three integration shards, and DOM
grants three staggered standalone unit suites eight workers each with no queue

rationale: The original three-lane fairness proof is superseded by the exact six-job topology plus the three-suite 24-core no-queue assertion; together they preserve and strengthen immediate concurrent start.

baseName: reserveVitestLaneBudget grants the FAIR core budget, not the single-process throttle

current.name: gives direct Vitest the same ledger-governed cap as the lane orchestrator
grants three staggered standalone unit suites eight workers each with no queue

rationale: The replacement asserts both the exact eight-worker fair reservation and parity between direct Vitest and the lane orchestrator, excluding the former single-process throttle.

baseName: reserveVitestLaneBudget REUSES a parent grant (preserves run-v2 ledger behavior)

current.name: reuses a parent grant without registering a second ledger entry

rationale: This is a direct semantic rename: the current assertion still proves parent grant reuse and explicitly checks that no duplicate ledger reservation is registered.

baseName: POST /api/maintenance/cleanup-worktrees rejects itemIds without mode without cleaning legacy orphans

current.name: rejects itemIds without mode

rationale: The renamed test remains the itemIds-without-mode validation case and compares the shared legacy orphan's path, branch, worktree list, and API inventory with its beforeAll baseline, preserving the original non-destruction assertion while avoiding a per-test fixture rebuild.

baseName: POST /api/maintenance/cleanup-worktrees rejects non-object bodies without cleaning legacy orphans

current.name: rejects every non-object body

rationale: The renamed table-driven test sends every original non-object body shape and verifies the shared legacy orphan snapshot is unchanged after each rejection, retaining the full validation and non-destruction semantics without rebuilding the fixture.

baseName: archived session worktree maintenance

current.name: GET /api/maintenance/archived-session-worktrees returns sessions, flattened items, groups, presets, and additive counts
scan exposes UX dispositions, reason categories, groups, and selection presets
POST /api/maintenance/cleanup-archived-session-worktrees validates request shape
scan and selected cleanup remove archived worktree while preserving archived session visibility
already-cleaned archived worktrees are hidden by default and stale selected cleanup is non-destructive
branch deletion is blocked when another archived record in the same repo references the branch
stale archived paths do not inherit git metadata from another worktree on the same branch
stale existing directories with colliding basenames are non-actionable
worktree key selectors must match the supplied session id
mode all cleans only safe candidates and leaves ineligible archived records untouched
sandbox container paths are skipped instead of treated as host worktrees
multi-repo archived sessions expose one worktree row per repoWorktrees entry
scan skips archived worktree paths still referenced by a live session

rationale: The original archived-worktree suite was split by behavior into scan, cleanup, guard, and selector files. Its thirteen child declarations are the precise static semantic owners of the original suite wrapper and collectively retain the complete suite coverage.

baseName: GET /api/maintenance/archived-session-worktrees returns sessions, flattened items, groups, presets, and additive counts

current.name: GET /api/maintenance/archived-session-worktrees returns sessions, flattened items, groups, presets, and additive counts

rationale: This scan-shape declaration moved unchanged into the focused archived-scan suite and continues to validate the complete response shape.

baseName: scan exposes UX dispositions, reason categories, groups, and selection presets

current.name: scan exposes UX dispositions, reason categories, groups, and selection presets

rationale: This declaration and its behavioral matrix moved unchanged into the focused archived-scan suite, preserving disposition, reason, group, and preset coverage.

baseName: POST /api/maintenance/cleanup-archived-session-worktrees validates request shape

current.name: POST /api/maintenance/cleanup-archived-session-worktrees validates request shape

rationale: This request-validation declaration moved unchanged into the archived-scan suite and still covers missing mode, mixed selectors, empty selected mode, invalid all-mode selectors, and valid all mode.

baseName: scan and selected cleanup remove archived worktree while preserving archived session visibility

current.name: scan and selected cleanup remove archived worktree while preserving archived session visibility

rationale: This end-to-end cleanup declaration moved unchanged into the focused archived-cleanup suite and retains worktree removal, branch deletion, response accounting, and archived-session visibility assertions.

baseName: already-cleaned archived worktrees are hidden by default and stale selected cleanup is non-destructive

current.name: already-cleaned archived worktrees are hidden by default and stale selected cleanup is non-destructive

rationale: This declaration moved unchanged into the archived-cleanup suite and preserves default filtering, diagnostic visibility, stale selection accounting, and branch non-destruction checks.

baseName: branch deletion is blocked when another archived record in the same repo references the branch

current.name: branch deletion is blocked when another archived record in the same repo references the branch

rationale: This shared-branch safety declaration moved unchanged into the archived-cleanup suite and still verifies cleanup removes only the worktree while retaining the referenced branch.

baseName: stale archived paths do not inherit git metadata from another worktree on the same branch

current.name: stale archived paths do not inherit git metadata from another worktree on the same branch

rationale: This path-association guard declaration moved unchanged into the focused archived-guards suite and continues to prove the active worktree and shared branch are untouched.

baseName: stale existing directories with colliding basenames are non-actionable

current.name: stale existing directories with colliding basenames are non-actionable

rationale: This basename-collision guard declaration moved unchanged into the archived-guards suite and retains scan classification, cleanup rejection, and filesystem/branch preservation assertions.

baseName: worktree key selectors must match the supplied session id

current.name: worktree key selectors must match the supplied session id

rationale: This selector integrity declaration moved unchanged into the archived-guards suite and still verifies mismatched session IDs are reported as invalid selections without deleting the worktree or branch.

baseName: mode all cleans only safe candidates and leaves ineligible archived records untouched

current.name: mode all cleans only safe candidates and leaves ineligible archived records untouched

rationale: This all-mode selection declaration moved unchanged into the focused archived-selectors suite and retains the removable, live-referenced, sandbox, stale, and already-cleaned outcome matrix.

baseName: sandbox container paths are skipped instead of treated as host worktrees

current.name: sandbox container paths are skipped instead of treated as host worktrees

rationale: This container-path safety declaration moved unchanged into the archived-selectors suite and preserves scan classification plus non-actionable cleanup-result coverage.

baseName: multi-repo archived sessions expose one worktree row per repoWorktrees entry

current.name: multi-repo archived sessions expose one worktree row per repoWorktrees entry

rationale: This multi-repository projection declaration moved unchanged into the archived-selectors suite and retains per-repository paths, metadata, statuses, and selection-category assertions.

baseName: scan skips archived worktree paths still referenced by a live session

current.name: scan skips archived worktree paths still referenced by a live session

rationale: This live-reference guard declaration moved unchanged into the archived-selectors suite and continues to require the ineligible referenced-record classification and non-actionable selection flags.

baseName: POST resolve returns real local diff cards

current.name: POST resolve returns local diff cards from the injected git boundary

rationale: The renamed test replaces process-backed Git setup with the injected git-diff boundary while preserving the local changeset ID, provider, files-changed count, card count, and changed-file diff-block assertions.

baseName: newWorktree=true allocates a distinct worktree/branch; newWorktree=false reuses the source worktree

current.name: newWorktree selects repo provisioning while reuse selects the source cwd

rationale: The replacement moves allocation behind the fork-create seam but retains both decisions: newWorktree requests repository worktree provisioning and yields a cwd distinct from the source, while reuse omits worktree options and keeps the source cwd.

baseName: newWorktree=true rebases cloned runtime cwd metadata off a stale source worktree

current.name: newWorktree rebases cloned runtime cwd metadata off a stale source cwd

rationale: The seam-based rename preserves the stale-runtime regression: cloned transcript content remains intact, stale system/session cwd records disappear, and both records are rewritten to the newly resolved cwd.

baseName: S-08: session in git repo gets a worktree

current.name: S-08: session in a detected repository selects worktree provisioning

rationale: The renamed story replaces real Git provisioning with the route decision seam while preserving the repository-detection contract: a worktree-enabled session request succeeds for the project and passes the detected repository as worktree provisioning options.
